name: guru
description: Search and read Guru knowledge-base cards and collections, and create or verify cards

envVars.name: GURU_USERNAME
envVars.description: HTTP Basic username: your Guru user email (for a User token) or the collection ID (for a Collection token)

envVars.name: GURU_USER_TOKEN
envVars.description: HTTP Basic password: the Guru User or Collection API token generated in API Access settings

tools.name: Search Cards
tools.description: Search the knowledge base for cards matching search terms

searchTerms.description: Search terms to match against card titles and content

maxResults.description: Maximum number of cards to return (up to 50; more results are paginated via the Link header)

showArchived.description: Include archived cards in the results

sortField.description: Sort field, e.g. lastModified, dateCreated, popularity (default is relevancy)

tools.name: Get Card
tools.description: Get a single card with its full content and additional details (collection, verifiers, collaborators)

cardId.description: The ID (or slug) of the card

tools.name: List Collections
tools.description: List the collections in the team to find collection IDs, names, and stats

tools.name: Create Card
tools.description: Create a new knowledge-base card in a collection

content.description: The card's HTML or Markdown content

collection.description: Collection to create the card in, e.g. { "id": "<collection id>" } (optional, but shared cards live in a collection)

shareStatus.description: Card visibility: PRIVATE, TEAM, or PUBLIC (the API defaults to PRIVATE when omitted, making the card visible only to the owner)

verificationInterval.description: Time interval in days indicating how often the card needs re-verification

tools.name: Update Card
tools.description: Update an existing card's title, content, or visibility to keep knowledge current

cardId.description: The ID of the card to update

content.description: The card's full HTML or Markdown content (replaces the existing content)

shareStatus.description: Card visibility: PRIVATE, TEAM, or PUBLIC (omitting it restricts the card to the owner)

verificationInterval.description: Time interval in days indicating how often the card needs re-verification (defaults to 30 when omitted)

tags.description: Tag objects to keep on the card — omitting tags or passing an empty list removes all existing tags

tools.name: Verify Card
tools.description: Mark a card as verified, confirming its content is accurate and up to date

cardId.description: The ID of the card to verify

prompts.title: Find an answer
prompts.prompt: Search my Guru knowledge base for cards about a topic I specify and summarize the verified answer.

prompts.title: Capture new knowledge
prompts.prompt: Turn the information I give you into a well-structured Guru card and create it in the collection I specify.

prompts.title: Verify a card
prompts.prompt: Look up a Guru card I specify, check whether its content still looks accurate, and verify it if so.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Guru setup

steps.title: Get a Guru account
steps.description: Sign up at https://www.getguru.com — a free trial workspace is enough for testing the API.

steps.title: Generate an API token
steps.description: In Guru, open Settings → API Access and generate a token. A User token (username = your email) acts as you across the knowledge base; a Collection token (username = collection ID) gives read-only access to one collection. Admins can issue tokens for other users.

steps.title: Store the credentials
steps.description: Add GURU_USERNAME=<your email or collection ID> and GURU_USER_TOKEN=<your token> to your .env file. The API uses HTTP Basic auth against https://api.getguru.com/api/v1.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the List Collections tool (or GET /api/v1/teams with curl) — a successful response confirms your credentials work.

notes: Collection tokens are read-only and scoped to a single collection — use a User token for the Create Card and Verify Card tools
Card search returns at most 50 results per page; narrow your search terms instead of paging when possible
shareStatus must be TEAM for a card to be visible to the whole team
